data.title: MSTR, CRWD, MRNA, GTLB On Traders' Radar Today — Dow Futures Edge Higher As Investors Look Past Middle East Conflict
data.description:
Key points:
  • Crude oil prices continued to boil on Wednesday amid the ongoing U.S.-Israel-Iran war.
  • President Trump stated that the United States will ensure the free flow of energy to the world.
  • He added that if necessary, the U.S. Navy will begin escorting tankers through the Strait of Hormuz as soon as possible.

U.S. stock futures edged higher early Wednesday as investors looked past the ongoing war in the Middle East.

This comes after markets whipsawed following the joint U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran that began during the weekend, with the war now in its fifth day. 

Following warnings from Iran over the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, President Donald Trump announced that the U.S. would extend risk insurance for maritime trade through the strait.

“If necessary, the United States Navy will begin escorting tankers through the Strait of Hormuz, as soon as possible. No matter what, the United States will ensure the FREE FLOW of ENERGY to the WORLD,” President Trump said in a post on Truth Social in a bid to allay concerns.

As of 5:30 a.m. ET on Monday, S&P futures rose 0.13%, Dow futures edged up by 0.03%, and Nasdaq futures rose 0.22%, while Russell 2000 futures gained 0.34%.

Meanwhile, retail sentiment toward the SPDR S&P 500 ETF (SPY) trended in the ‘neutral’ territory, while the Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) ETF was in the ‘bearish’ territory at the time of writing.

Crude Oil Prices Continue To Boil

Crude oil prices continued to boil on Wednesday amid the ongoing U.S.-Israel-Iran war.

At the time of writing, U.S. West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude futures maturing in April were up 1.05%, rising to $75.34 per barrel, compared to the previous closing price of $74.56.

Brent crude futures maturing in May gained 1.77%, rising to $82.84 per barrel.

Trending Stocks To Watch

Strategy (MSTR), Robinhood (HOOD), Coinbase (COIN): Shares of Strategy, Robinhood, and Coinbase rose in Wednesday’s pre-market trade following a rise in Bitcoin prices. Bitcoin was up nearly 7% over the past 24 hours, hovering at $71,400.

CrowdStrike Holdings (CRWD): CrowdStrike shares gained after the company’s fourth-quarter results beat Wall Street expectations. The cybersecurity company’s first-quarter revenue guidance was also ahead of estimates.

Moderna (MRNA): Moderna shares jumped nearly 6% in Wednesday’s pre-market trade after the company entered into a settlement deal over a vaccine patent dispute with Genevant Sciences and Arbutus Biopharma.

GitLab (GTLB): GitLab shares tumbled nearly 7% pre-market after the company’s fiscal year 2027 earnings guidance of $0.76 to $0.8 per share came in considerably lower than Wall Street estimates of an earnings per share of $1.03.

United States Oil Fund (USO), ProShares Ultra Bloomberg Crude Oil (UCO): Crude oil ETFs continued to rise early Wednesday amid the Middle East conflict.

data.title: The 10-Point: The Wall Street Journal's Guide to the Day's Top News
data.description:
By Emma Tucker

The war in the Middle East continues to escalate as U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran draw intensifying retaliatory missile and drone barrages. Financial markets from Seoul to New York slumped on inflation fears as oil prices soared. We're covering the fallout from the front lines: Our visual journalists take you inside the operation that killed Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, while our senior markets columnist, James Mackintosh, explains how oil's volatility will dictate the winners and losers on Wall Street — for now.

Today's Headlines

James Talarico defeated Jasmine Crockett in the Democratic Senate primary in Texas, as his message of religious faith and economic populism resonated with Hispanic voters whom Democrats hope to pull back from President Trump.

A Kuwaiti jet fighter was the cause of the accidental shootdown of three American F-15s on Sunday, initial reports say.

Israel's military is targeting the Iranian police state that brutally suppressed protests and killed thousands of people, with the hope of clearing the way for a popular revolt to overthrow the Islamic government.

The Justice Department has withheld thousands of documents from the Epstein files, including FBI documents that detailed a woman's unverified allegations of sexual misconduct against President Trump.

Live From The Markets

American frackers aren't rushing to pump more oil despite the market chaos.

In a day of wild market moves, oil is a new haven, writes James Mackintosh.

The U.S.-Israeli attacks against Iran have halted a weekslong rally in U.S. government bonds.

Read It Here First

See inside the operation that killed Khamenei.

Iran's leadership didn't see the strike coming. On Saturday morning, Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei — who typically went down into his bunkers at night when the regime believed Israeli attacks were likely to take place — was above ground at his residence. The unexpected daylight attack killed Khamenei and was the opening blow in Israel and the U.S.'s war with Iran. The WSJ reconstructed the operation through interviews with current and former Israeli and U.S. officials, as well as satellite imagery, videos and photos. Reporting was by Anat Peled, Milàn Czerny, Dov Lieber and Anika Arora Seth.

Powell won, but the Fed might still lose.

For eight years, Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's rule for dealing with Trump was simple: Don't make eye contact. Then, on a Sunday night in January, he decided to look the president straight in the face. After receiving subpoenas concerning his testimony months earlier about the Fed's building renovations, Powell — whose term as chair ends in May — released a bold video dismissing that explanation. His gambit had its intended effect, rallying bipartisan support to the Fed, which, for now, has preserved its independence. But even those who cheered his defiance in the skirmish aren't sure the bank can win a longer war against sustained presidential pressure, reports Nick Timiraos.

Expert Take

Q: How is Congress challenging Trump's strikes on Iran?

Most Republicans on Capitol Hill have backed Trump's strikes against Iran and defended his decision to bypass Congress. But some say the president would need to seek authorization from Congress if hostilities drag on, or if he wants to send in ground troops, report Lindsay Wise, Anvee Bhutani and Siobhan Hughes.

A: Later this week, the GOP-led Senate and House are expected to defeat a pair of war-power resolutions that would rein in Trump's military operations against Iran, rejecting an effort led by Democrats and a few Republicans. Nor is Trump expected to ask lawmakers for a vote to approve his use of force, even though the Constitution assigns to Congress the power to declare war.

Some Republicans have indicated that their support for the U.S. war in Iran isn't unconditional or indefinite. As the war intensified across the Middle East, GOP lawmakers said there could eventually come a point when the Trump administration should formally seek authorization from Congress.

Republican leaders expressed confidence that they could block the war powers resolutions, which would require Trump to remove U.S. troops from hostilities against Iran without explicit approval from Congress. Republicans narrowly control the House, 218-214, and have a 53-47 edge in the Senate.

Sen. Tim Kaine (D., Va.) has acknowledged the resolution is unlikely to pass. Still, he said that the unease and disquiet on the Republican side is growing and Democrats plan to force more war-powers votes in the future, even if this one fails.

See The Story

China's 90-second dramas are taking over U.S. phones.

The Journal goes on the set of "Vicious," a microdrama at the center of a fast-growing format disrupting content streaming in the U.S.

Happening Today

Economic data: ADP's National Employment Report for February

Earnings: Okta, Broadcom, StubHub

Number Of The Day:

>2,000

The number of lawsuits on the Court of International Trade's doorstep as businesses fight to recover tariffs paid under the Trump administration. More are expected. The trade court ruled last year that Trump didn't have the authority to impose tariffs on nearly every nation, and the Supreme Court agreed — but said nothing about what should happen next. Now the obscure court, which typically handles fewer than 300 cases annually, is facing a rare moment in the sun as it weighs potential refunds.

And Finally...

These alarm clocks shock you, make you do math and take your money.

Product designers are coming up with ways to rouse deep sleepers that border on diabolical. Pavlok's Shock Clock 3 goes for $159.99 and uses an electric shock sequence to force users out of bed. Wake-up app Alarmy has its roughly 900,000 daily U.S. users complete "missions" such as doing squats, shaking their phone or typing out motivational quotes before it stops sounding.

data.title: Britain fines Wood Group $17 million for inaccurate results
data.description:
Britain's Financial Conduct Authority fined Wood Group nearly 13 million pounds ($17.39 million) on Wednesday for publishing inaccurate information in its financial results for 2022, 2023 and the first half of 2025.

The financial watchdog said following the poor performance of certain projects, the company's accounting judgments were inappropriately influenced by a desire to maintain previously stated financial results and that it did not have adequate systems or controls to prevent this.

Wood Group's issues came to light from November 2024, triggering a 85% plunge in its share price by April 2025 and leading to a share suspension the following month. The company agreed to a $292 million takeover by Dubai-based Sidara later in the year.

Sidara had tabled a lowered bid for Wood Group in August 2025, following the regulatory investigation and had made the deal's completion conditional on the British company publishing its delayed 2024 results and ensuring that certain debt facilities were not terminated.

Wood Group met the regulatory conditions for the deal earlier this week and expects to close the transaction on March 10.

($1 = 0.7477 pounds)

data.title: DAX Up Over 1.5% As Stocks Rebound After Recent Tumble
data.description:
BRUSSELS (dpa-AFX) - Despite rising tensions in the Middle East, German stocks moved higher on Wednesday, due largely to some bargain hunting after two successive days of sharp declines.

U.S. President Donald Trump's announcement that the U.S. Navy would escort oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz to safeguard maritime trade in the Gulf and help stabilize surging global energy prices, is aiding sentiment a bit.

The U.S. Development Finance Corporation (DFC) has confirmed its readiness to extend political risk insurance and guarantees for energy shipments passing through the Gulf.

The benchmark DAX was up 369.98 points or 1.56% at 24,123.00 slightly past noon.

Infineon Technologies and Daimler Truck Holding are up 4.4% and 4.2%, respectively. Rheinmetall, Siemens Energy, Siemens Healthineers and Siemens are up 2.3%-2.5%.

BASF, Deutsche Post, Munich RE, Henkel, Merck, Volkswagen, Allianz, Zalando, SAP, Hannover Rueck, Deutsche Bank, Mercedes-Benz, BMW, Porsche Automobil Holding and Gea group are gaining 1%-2.2%.

Adidas is plunging 7.4% after announcing changes in its supervisory board.

Bayer is declining by nearly 2%. Brenntag is down 1.1%. The German flavors and fragrances maker Symrise is down 1.4% after forecasting a low single-digit decline in first-quarter organic sales.

Data from S&P Global showed the HCOB Germany Composite PMI was revised slightly higher to 53.2 in February 2026 from a preliminary of 53.1, and above 52.1 in January. It is the highest reading in four months, amid faster increases in activity in both the manufacturing and service sectors.

The HCOB Germany Services PMI was revised slightly higher to a four-month high of 53.5 in February 2026 from a preliminary of 53.4, and compared to 52.4 in January, final figures showed.

The HCOB Eurozone Composite PMI climbed to 51.9 in February 2026, up from 51.3 in January, marking the strongest expansion in private sector activity in three months. The HCOB Eurozone Services PMI inched higher to 51.9 in February of 2026 from 51.6 in the previous month, slightly ahead of the preliminary estimate of 51.8 and in line with the initial market expectations.

Industrial producer prices in the Euro Area rose 0.7% month over month in January 2026, reversing a 0.3% decline in December and surpassing market expectations of a 0.2% increase. On an annual basis, producer prices were 2.1% lower than a year earlier, following a 2.0% year-on-year decrease in December.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: Russia prepared to divert oil to India as Middle East conflict disrupts flows, source says
data.description:
Key points:
  • Fleet with 9.5 mln barrels of Russian crude near Indian waters, says source
  • Russia ready to raise Indian crude import share to 40% from 30% Russia set to sell LNG to India after Qatar output stopped-source

By Krishna N. Das and Nidhi Verma

Russia is ready to divert oil to India to offset Middle East supply disruptions, with about 9.5 million barrels of Russian crude in vessels near Indian waters and able to arrive within weeks, an industry source with direct knowledge told Reuters.

The source declined to say where the non‑Russian fleet cargoes were originally headed but said they could deliver to India within weeks, giving refiners rapid relief.

India is vulnerable to supply shocks, with crude stocks covering only about 25 days of demand, while refiners hold similarly limited inventories of gasoil, gasoline and liquefied petroleum gas.

An Indian government source said New Delhi was scouting for alternative supply to prepare for continuing conflict in the Middle East beyond 10–15 days.

FORCED TO SEEK ALTERNATIVE SUPPLY

The disruption has immediate market consequences, with about 40% of India's crude imports moving through the Strait of Hormuz, the world's most vital oil export route, the source said, and the near-closure of the route has compelled the No.3 oil consumer to seek alternatives.

Indian refiners process about 5.6 million barrels per day of crude. The Strait has become inaccessible after vessels were struck by Iranian attacks that followed U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran-based targets that commenced on Saturday.

The industry source, speaking on condition of anonymity, said Russia was ready to help India meet up to 40% of its crude needs.

India's imports of Russian crude fell to about 1.1 million barrels per day in January, the lowest since November 2022, as New Delhi sought relief from U.S. tariffs, pushing Moscow’s share of overall oil imports down to 21.2%, industry data showed. The source said the share climbed back to around 30% in February.

Indian refiners are in regular contact with traders selling Russian crude, but any increase in intake from Moscow would depend on guidance from the government as trade talks with the United States continue, two refining sources said.

U.S. President Donald Trump last month agreed to drop punitive tariffs levied on imports from India over its purchase of Russian oil, saying New Delhi had agreed to "stop buying Russian oil."

India has not done so, insisting its strategy was to diversify supply in line with market conditions and "evolving international dynamics."

India's foreign and oil ministries, as well as the Russian embassy in New Delhi, did not immediately respond to a request for comment on any higher purchases from Russia. An Indian source said days before the Iran war that Indian companies had not been told to shun Russian oil.

'A SELLER'S MARKET' FOR OIL

While Russian oil has been sold at a discount to global prices since the country invaded Ukraine in 2022, that will now narrow as "it's become a sellers' market," the industry source with knowledge of Russian oil trade said.

The source said Russia was also ready to sell liquefied natural gas to India after top supplier Qatar halted production on Monday as the conflict widened.

Indian companies have reduced gas supplies to some industrial customers to manage the shortfall, Reuters has reported.

Both China and India, Asia's biggest energy consumers, source about half their crude imports from the Middle East, but India holds far less in storage than China and is more exposed to regional supply shocks as Russian purchases fell under U.S. pressure.

Trump said on Tuesday the U.S. Navy could escort oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz if necessary, and ordered the U.S. International Development Finance Corporation to provide political risk insurance and guarantees for Gulf shipping.

data.title: Italy’s MPS adds governance risk to dicey M&A
data.description:
By Neil Unmack

Luigi Lovaglio, CEO of Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena, may not be on a list of candidates for the new board. It comes months after his complex $19 bln Mediobanca takeover, and following a clash with a top shareholder. The disruption raises more doubts over a tough integration.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow @Unmack1 on X

CONTEXT NEWS

Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena CEO Luigi Lovaglio will not be included in a list of candidates for the renewal of the lender's board, Reuters reported on March 4, citing two people familiar with the matter.

The move comes after a clash with top shareholder Francesco Gaetano Caltagirone over the delisting of Mediobanca, the bank it acquired but which is still listed, Reuters reported.

A list of 20 candidates will be approved later on March 4, Reuters reported.

Monte dei Paschi shares fell nearly 3% on March 4, to 7.58 euros, as of 1045 GMT.

data.title: Gran Tierra Energy 2025 10-K: $596.7M Revenue, $(5.45) EPS
data.description:
Gran Tierra Energy reported 2025 oil, natural gas and NGL sales of $596.7 million and a net loss of $193.1 million, or $(5.45) per share, driven largely by a $136.3 million non‑cash ceiling test impairment and higher operating and DD&A costs. Revenue declined 4% year‑over‑year primarily from a 15% drop in Brent prices and changes in sales mix. The company's gross profit contracted to $66.4 million and operating (loss) before income taxes swung to $(232.9) million in 2025.

Financial Highlights

  • Oil, natural gas and NGL sales: $596.7 million (2025), down 4% YoY due to 15% lower Brent and sales mix changes.
  • Gross profit: $66.4 million (2025), decreased 64% YoY primarily from higher operating and DD&A costs.
  • Operating income (loss) before income taxes: $(232.9) million (2025) versus $44.6 million income in 2024.
  • Net loss: $(193.1) million (2025), which includes a $136.3 million non‑cash ceiling test impairment.
  • Net loss per share: $(5.45) per share basic and diluted (2025).

Business Highlights

  • Production growth: 2025 NAR production rose 38% to 38,443 BOEPD, driven by Ecuador exploration success and full‑year Canada operations.
  • Revenue mix shift: Colombia's share of revenue fell to 70% (2024: 93%), Canada contributed 19% and Ecuador 11%, changing the geographic balance of sales.
  • Operational efficiency: Operating expense per boe improved 10% to $18.09 despite higher total operating costs from Ecuador ramp‑up and Canada full‑year activity.
  • Capital program & execution: 2026 capital expenditure guidance of $120–160 million (approximately 90% development), with planned allocation of ~50% to Colombia, ~35% to Canada and ~15% to Ecuador; 2026 drilling is already underway.
  • Logistics & sales channels: Increased pipeline transport to 46% of sales in 2025 and a strategic prepayment oil delivery agreement covering Ecuador (expanded to include Colombia), affecting sales routing.

Original SEC Filing: GRAN TIERRA ENERGY INC. [ GTE ] - 10-K - Mar. 04, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Gold gains as Middle East conflict revives safe-haven bid
data.description:
Key points:
  • Gold rises above 2%, silver up over 5%
  • Platinum up 5%, Palladium up over 3%
  • Oil, gas prices surge as Middle East energy shipments stall

By Pablo Sinha

Gold prices climbed 2% on Wednesday, rebounding from their lowest in more than a week reached in the previous session, as the dollar took a breather and mounting tensions in the Middle East drove investors toward safe havens.

Spot gold gained 2.2% to $5,198.58 per ounce by 1017 GMT, after falling more than 4% on Tuesday.

U.S. gold futures for April delivery added 1.7% to $5,211.20.

The U.S. dollar fell 0.2%, making greenback-priced gold more affordable for buyers using other currencies.

"After the past few days of position unwinds and dollar strength, markets are back to a more typical macro risk-off stance, with silver higher too. A pause in the rise of the dollar and Treasury yields helps with their opportunity costs," said Jamie Dutta, market analyst at Nemo.money.

"Gold and silver's safe-haven characteristics can shine again."

Gold's appeal as it draws support from the widening conflict in the Middle East is expected to remain intact even if some investors have favored the dollar as their preferred safe-haven, traders and analysts said on Tuesday.

Spot silver advanced 5.3% to $86.39 per ounce, after falling more than 8% in the last session.

U.S. forces continued round-the-clock assaults on Iran, and Israel mounted a "broad wave" of strikes targeting Iranian missile sites and air defense systems.

Asian stocks tanked as investors dumped crowded bets on chipmakers on worries that the widening Middle East war would drive an oil shock, accelerating inflation and delaying interest rate cuts.

Markets see the U.S. Federal Reserve holding rates at its two-day meeting later this month. (FEDWATCH)

"If the military campaign prolongs or expands across the region, safe-haven demand could continue to support gold above the $5,000/oz level and potentially open the door for a retest of the recent highs," said Linh Tran, senior market analyst at XS.com.

Spot platinum added 5.1% to $2,189.68 an ounce. The global platinum market is forecast to post a fourth consecutive year of deficit in 2026 at 240,000 troy ounces, the World Platinum Investment Council said. Palladium gained 3.6% to $1,705.71.

data.title: Hormuz reopening is not solely in Trump’s gift
data.description:
By Yawen Chen

The US president thinks tanker insurance and Navy escorts will restart Strait flows. But his fleets are targets, escorts lack ships, and precedent suggests shipping volumes don’t instantly recover from these sorts of shocks. Unless hostilities end soon, $100 oil looks likely.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow Yawen Chen on Bluesky and LinkedIn.

CONTEXT NEWS

The U.S. Navy could begin escorting oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz if necessary, President Donald Trump said on March 3, adding he had ordered the U.S. International Development Finance Corporation (DFC) to provide political risk insurance and financial guarantees for maritime trade in the Gulf.

The DFC said on March 3 it is ready to mobilize its Political Risk Insurance and Guaranty products to stabilize international commerce and support American and allied businesses operating in the Middle East during this period of conflict with the Iranian regime. Brent crude rose 3.3% to $84 a barrel by 0659 GMT on March 4, after closing on March 3 at its highest since January 2025.

data.title: GE Shipping expands fleet with new vessel buy
data.description:
https://www.cnbctv18.com/market/stocks/great-eastern-shipping-company-ltd-share-price/7I03QD/Great Eastern Shipping Company Ltd (GE Shipping) has contracted to acquire a secondhand Kamsarmax dry bulk carrier as part of its ongoing fleet expansion strategy.

In an exchange filing on March 4, 2026, the company said it has agreed to purchase a 2014 Japanese-built Kamsarmax vessel with a capacity of approximately 81,094 deadweight tonnes (dwt). The vessel is expected to join the company’s fleet by the first quarter of FY27.

GE Shipping said the purchase aligns with its strategy to expand and modernise its fleet.

Following this acquisition, the company’s owned fleet stands at 41 vessels, comprising 27 tankers, including five crude tankers, 17 product tankers and five LPG carriers, and 14 dry bulk carriers, which include two Capesize, nine Kamsarmax, one Ultramax and two Supramax vessels. The total fleet capacity aggregates to about 3.25 million dwt. The company’s current capacity utilisation is close to 100%.

Separately, GE Shipping has contracted to sell its Very Large Gas Carrier (VLGC) Jag Vishnu. The transaction is expected to be completed in the fourth quarter of FY26.

Q3 Results

GE Shipping reported a strong financial performance for the third quarter of FY26, with net profit rising 36.9% year-on-year to ₹812 crore, compared with ₹593 crore in the corresponding quarter last year.

Revenue for the quarter increased 17.6% year-on-year to ₹1,454 crore. Earnings before interest, tax, depreciation and amortisation (EBITDA) surged 36.7% to ₹835.2 crore from ₹611 crore a year ago.

Operating margin expanded sharply to 57.4% from 49.4% in the year-ago period, supported by firm freight rates and operating leverage.

The company’s board also declared a third interim dividend of ₹9 per equity share for FY26.

Shares of https://www.cnbctv18.com/market/stocks/great-eastern-shipping-company-ltd-share-price/7I03QD/Great Eastern Shipping Company Ltd ended higher on Wednesday, March 4, by 2.14% at ₹1354.80 on the NSE.

data.title: Eurozone Unemployment Rate Falls
data.description:
BRUSSELS/FRANKFURT/PARIS (dpa-AFX) - The euro area unemployment rate dropped slightly in January, Eurostat reported Wednesday.

The jobless rate fell to seasonally adjusted 6.1 percent from 6.2 percent in December. The rate was forecast to remain unchanged at 6.2 percent.

The number of unemployed people decreased by 184,000 from December. Compared to last year, unemployment fell 273,000 to 10.77 million.

There were 2.352 million young unemployed persons in January, which was down by 27,000 from December. The youth unemployment rate decreased to 14.8 percent from 15.0 percent in December.

Data showed that the unemployment rate in the EU27 came in at 5.8 percent compared to 5.9 percent in December.

data.title: National Vision 2025 10-K: $1.99B Revenue, $0.37 Diluted EPS
data.description:
National Vision reported fiscal 2025 net revenue of $1,987.5 million and diluted earnings per share of $0.37, reflecting a return to profitability after the prior-year loss. The company cited revenue growth driven by pricing, product mix and exam growth while adjusted diluted EPS from continuing operations was $0.80 on a non-GAAP basis. Net income for the year was $29.6 million, compared with a loss in the prior year.

Financial Highlights

  • Total net revenue: $1,987.5 million, up $164.2 million (9.0%) versus prior year (includes a 53-week year that added $35.6 million).
  • Gross profit: $1,168.0 million (reported as total net revenue minus total costs applicable to revenue).
  • Income (loss) from operations: $58.8 million (improved from a $(10.4) million loss in the prior year).
  • Net income: $29.6 million (prior year: $(28.5) million); discontinued operations contribution was $0 in 2025.
  • Diluted net income per share: $0.37; Adjusted diluted EPS from continuing operations (non-GAAP): $0.80. Weighted diluted shares: 80.576 million.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue drivers: Growth attributed to pricing and product mix improvements and increased eye exam volumes.
  • Channel & brand performance: Owned store portfolio totaled roughly 1,250 locations with America’s Best delivering 6.3% comparable-store growth.
  • Customer mix & managed care: Managed-care sales represented approximately 42% of revenue; a stronger managed-care cohort helped raise ticket value.
  • Operational modernization: Rolled out remote telemedicine services in over 800 locations and implemented in-store technology and brand refreshes during 2025.
  • Store expansion & capacity: Opened 33 new stores in 2025 and is targeting about 30–35 new America’s Best stores in 2026 to expand exam capacity.

Original SEC Filing: National Vision Holdings, Inc. [ EYE ] - 10-K - Mar. 04, 2026

data.title: Kontoor 2025 10-K: $3,152.5M Revenue, $4.05 EPS
data.description:
Kontoor reported fiscal 2025 net revenues of $3,152.5 million and diluted EPS of $4.05, reflecting a 21% revenue increase driven by the Helly Hansen acquisition and a $0.23 EPS contribution from that business. Gross margin improved to $1,423.4 million (45.2% of revenues), while operating income of $336.8 million and net income of $227.5 million were pressured by acquisition/integration, restructuring charges and a higher effective tax rate. The results include benefits from Project Jeanius and a ~2% tailwind from a 53rd week.

Financial Highlights

  • Net revenues: $3,152.5 million, up 21% versus 2024 (includes $475.5 million from Helly Hansen and ~2% benefit from a 53rd week).
  • Gross margin (net revenues less cost of goods sold): $1,423.4 million, 45.2% of revenues, up 70 basis points (attributed to mix, Helly Hansen and Project Jeanius).
  • Operating income: $336.8 million, 10.7% of revenues, down 2% versus 2024 (includes acquisition/integration and restructuring charges).
  • Net income: $227.5 million, decreased 7% versus 2024 (impacted by higher effective tax rate and after-tax charges).
  • Diluted earnings per share: $4.05, down from $4.36 in 2024; included a $0.23 contribution from Helly Hansen and a $1.54 after-tax charge impact.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers: Growth was largely driven by the integration of Helly Hansen and organic Wrangler momentum.
  • Channel performance: Direct-to-consumer sales rose 49%; international wholesale increased 65%; U.S. wholesale grew modestly at 7%.
  • Brand and portfolio: The additions of Helly Hansen and Musto expanded Kontoor's premium outdoor and workwear scale, diversifying the portfolio.
  • Operational transformation: Project Jeanius continued, including plant closures, supply-chain diversification and cost optimization initiatives.
  • Margin and cost actions: Gross margin gains were driven by favorable mix and benefits from Project Jeanius despite ongoing restructuring, tariff and product cost pressures.

Original SEC Filing: Kontoor Brands, Inc. [ KTB ] - 10-K - Mar. 04, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Black Rock Coffee Bar posts $200.3M revenue, $(0.00) EPS — 10-K summary
data.description:
Black Rock Coffee Bar reported total revenue of $200.3 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, a 24.5% increase year-over-year driven by store growth and same-store sales gains. The company recorded a net loss of $16.539 million (net loss per share reported as ($0.00) on 17,021,377 weighted-average shares) and operating income declined to $0.901 million, reflecting margin pressure despite higher revenue. Store-level profitability improved, with Store-Level Profit (presented as gross profit) rising to $58.492 million, supporting unit economics as the chain scales.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $200.321 million, up 24.5% year-over-year to Dec 31, 2025.
  • Gross Profit (Store-Level Profit): $58.492 million; Store-Level Profit Margin 29.2% (up from 27.9%).
  • Operating Income (Income from operations): $0.901 million; operating margin 0.4%, down from $6.033 million (3.7%) prior year.
  • Net loss (Net Income): $(16.539) million; net loss margin (8.3%) versus $(7.187) million prior year.
  • Net loss per share (Net loss per share of Class A common stock): ($0.00), based on 17,021,377 weighted-average shares.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue drivers: Growth driven by store expansion and a 10.1% same-store sales increase composed of traffic (+6.7%) and menu price/mix (+4.5%).
  • Store expansion: Opened 32 net new drive-thru focused stores, bringing the total to 181 locations and achieving more than 20% new unit growth target.
  • Unit economics: Store-Level Profit Margin improved to 29.2% from 27.9% due to operational discipline and scale efficiencies.
  • Operational capabilities: Approximately 73% of locations include lobbies; investments made in the digital platform, production facilities, and corporate buildout to support continued growth.

Original SEC Filing: Black Rock Coffee Bar, Inc. [ BRCB ] - 10-K - Mar. 04, 2026

data.title: 9000 Plus Americans Returned From The Middle East: State Dept.
data.description:
JERUSALEM (dpa-AFX) - U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio has announced that more than 9000 American citizens have safely returned from the Middle East, including more than 300 from Israel, since the start of the war.

'We have about 1,500 Americans that are requesting assistance with departure. We have identified and continued to identify charter flights, military flight options, and expanded commercial flight options, meaning working with the airlines to send bigger airplanes with more seats, and a combination of those three things,' he said at a news conference Tuesday.

'The impediment we're facing now, in many cases - we've had a couple instances in which we have planes in the air and on the way, and unfortunately the airspace gets closed and they have to turn back around. So we're working through those challenges,' he told reporters.

In a message to Americans who are in the Middle East and in need of assistance, Rubio said that it is important to 'put both the website and the phone numbers on your screens, because we need to know where you are'. 'We need to have contact information for Americans that need assistance. They have to register with us, because as these options begin to open up, we have to be able to call you and know where you're staying so we can get this information to you and coordinate appropriately'.

He added that stranded Anericans will be evacuated using a variety of methods: 'charter flights, military flights and transports, expanded commercial opportunities, and in some cases land routes that will allow them to go to neighboring countries who might have open airports at that point'.

Separately, the U.S. Department of State said it is facilitating charter flights from the United Arab Emirates, Saudi Arabia, and Jordan for American citizens, and will continue to secure additional capacity as security conditions allow.

Commercial aviation options remain available in Saudi Arabia, UAE, Oman, and Egypt, and the Department is actively helping American citizens book those tickets. For those in countries lacking commercial aviation availability, the Department is facilitating travel to third countries as conditions allow. That includes increasing ground transportation options for American citizens wishing to leave Israel.

The State Department said it has answered calls from nearly 3000 American citizens in the Middle East and continues to provide travel options for those wishing to leave. The Department will also waive any statutory requirement for American citizens to reimburse the government for travel expenses.

data.title: European Wax Center revenue falls, to be taken private
data.description:
Overview

  • Waxing services provider's fiscal 2025 revenue decreased 4.7% yr/yr

  • Adjusted net income for fiscal 2025 beat analyst expectations

  • Company to be taken private by General Atlantic in all-cash deal

Outlook

  • European Wax Center will not provide fiscal 2026 guidance due to privatization

Result Drivers

  • SERVICE MIX SHIFT - System-wide sales decreased due to a shift in service mix

  • SG&A EXPENSES - Increase in SG&A expenses driven by strategic investments and one-time support to franchisees

  • CENTER CLOSURES - Franchisee center closures contributed to decline in system-wide sales

  • The average consensus recommendation for the personal services peer group is "buy."

data.title: MAMK: FY2025 revenue rose 36%, but net income dropped 99% as margins compressed and costs increased
data.description:
Revenue grew 36% to $29.2M in FY2025, but net income plunged to $18K due to lower margins and higher costs. Gross margin fell to 8.95% as domestic sales rose and overseas demand weakened. Trading in A Shares was suspended on Nasdaq pending regulatory review.

data.title: WIX: Double-digit growth in 2025, with Base44 and Harmony fueling momentum into 2026
data.description:
Q4 and full year 2025 saw double-digit revenue and bookings growth, driven by strong performance in new cohorts, Base44, and Harmony. 2026 guidance calls for continued mid-teens growth and significant investment in innovation, with a $2B share repurchase and $250M equity raise supporting long-term strategy.
